acceptance_criteria: expert_memo.md is untruncated and maps every computed value to spreadsheet cell addresses
privacy_release_3.xlsx includes per-row flags and formulas and is parseable by openpyxl OR a formulas.txt/CSV export is included that reproduces formulas
Reproducibility checklist completed and documents rounding/needs_review items
Quantified validation plan for recommended option included
extra_model_fields.notes: Prioritize exposing formulas and cell addresses and making artifacts machine-readable. The grader already judged arithmetic correct; focus is on reproducibility and metadata consistency.
verification_steps: Run openpyxl load test on privacy_release_3.xlsx in an environment matching verifier; if fails, generate formulas.txt and include it in deliverables.
Run deterministic precheck to confirm artifact_contract_passed and artifact parseable.
Re-run model grader and verifier; confirm no internal inconsistencies in their JSON reports.

planned_changes: Untruncate expert_memo.md to include full reproducibility checklist and an explicit mapping from each reported value to its spreadsheet cell address (e.g., Packet!B2 -> R1.cell_count, Calculations!C4 -> avg_cell_count).

Embed the actual spreadsheet formulas for all intermediate computations in the memo (copy-paste formula strings) and/or generate and include a formulas.txt export listing cell addresses and formulas. Attach this file to the attempt deliverables.

Add per-record flag columns in privacy_release_3.xlsx (e.g., suppression_flag, privacy_review_flag) with formulas implementing rules (suppression_flag = cell_count<5, privacy_review_flag = epsilon_requested>1). In the memo, cite exact cell addresses for these flags and show sample evaluation for each record.

Ensure privacy_release_3.xlsx is programmatically parseable in the verifier environment: run an environment check using openpyxl and, if not available, include an alternate machine-readable CSV/formula export that expresses the same formulas and values.

Document rounding and handling of fractional values (e.g., post-remediation cell_count fractional handling and whether counts are rounded or left fractional) and mark any unresolved choices as needs_review.
Quantify validation burden for top recommendation (O3): provide estimated expert-hours, minimal test cases to validate change, and a one-paragraph validation procedure in the memo.

Fix the grader-report inconsistency before resubmission: ensure failed_criteria, passed_criteria, and rubric_item_scores agree on ri_5 status; include a short note in the attempt metadata summarizing changes made to address verifier complaints.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Resolve reproducibility and verification inconsistencies so rubric requirements (especially ri_5) are demonstrably satisfied and automated parsing/verification succeed.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline submission produced correct arithmetic and a reasonable recommendation (O3) but has reproducibility and reporting issues. The XLSX could not be parsed in the verification environment (openpyxl_unavailable) and the memo preview is truncated and does not expose explicit spreadsheet formulas, cell addresses, or completed reproducibility checklist items as required by the rubric. The verifier also found an internal inconsistency in the grader report regarding ri_5 (passed vs failed). Fixing these gaps is needed to fully satisfy reproducibility and rubric consistency requirements.
